thread.title_full: Bairwa Accelerates Higher Education Reforms and Foreign Study Scholarships in Rajasthan | Jaipur News - Times of India

title: Bairwa Accelerates Higher Education Reforms and Foreign Study Scholarships in Rajasthan | Jaipur News - Times of India
text: Jaipur: Deputy chief minister Prem Chand Bairwa on Friday directed officials to expedite the revision of curricula in higher education institutions to meet contemporary needs and emphasised the urgent need for reforms in the state's higher education sector.Chairing a crucial review meeting with officials of the department at Shiksha Sankul, Bairwa also issued instructions to end the delay in implementing the Swami Vivekanand Scholarship for Academic Excellence scheme, which funds education abroad for 300 students annually. He instructed officials to fast-track the process of releasing the list of successful candidates and ensure timely fee payments for students already enrolled abroad, said an official.The delay in executing the scheme, introduced in Mar, has drawn wide criticism. With only 67 students sent abroad so far, many aspiring candidates have missed the opportunity to study in foreign universities. Aspiring students are turning to Bairwa and officials, including department secretary Arushi Malik, but they are unable to meet them, said officials.Malik was absent from the meeting due to official commitments. \"Officials have been asked to meet the students and parents and streamline the process so that they do not suffer further,\" said an official.Bairwa also expressed concern over the low gross enrolment ratio in higher education institutions and urged officials to increase enrolment figures. He instructed the commissioner of colleges to design programmes fostering innovation in the curricula.Bairwa also reviewed the progress on infrastructure development in newly established colleges and urged officials to prioritise timely completion of these projects. He stressed that all schemes and initiatives under the department must be implemented in a time-bound manner to ensure tangible improvements in the state's higher education landscape.\"Building a scientific temperament is essential for students' growth and the development of a forward-thinking academic ecosystem,\" said Bairwa at the meeting.
